senses.gloss: necessary, indispensable, unavoidable, essential, inevitable, inescapable, requisite

etymology.text: Derived from the active participle of the verb وَجَبَ (wajaba, “to be necessary”).

body.text: (TA.) So in the following verse of Keys Ibn-ElKhateem:

A^aTaAEato banuw EawofK A^amiyrFA nahaAhumu
                      
                     
                        Eani Als~ilomi Hat~aY kaAna A^aw~ala waAjibi
The sons of 'Owf obeyed a commander who forbade them to make peace until he was the first who was slain,
In the science of the fundamentals of religion,
existing; of which the nonexistence cannot be mentally conceived:
as the essence of God. (IbrD.)] ―         -b2-  Accord. to [the Imám] Aboo-Haneefeh,
[in matters of religion] is not so strong a term as
indispensable, duty; yet not so decisively
manifestly shown to be such as that which is termed
:] or, accord. to Esh-Sháfi'ee, these two terms are syn., signifying [
obligatory, by God's express appointment, as
a thing for neglecting which one will be punished:
that which should be preferred and approved;
thus explained by ElKhattábee as occurring in the following trad.:

body.text_bw: gusolu AljumoEapi waAjibN EalaY kul~i muHotalimK
body.text_ar: غُسْلُ الجُمْعَةِ وَاجِبٌ عَلَى كُلِّ مُحْتَلِمٍ

body.text: The ablution prescribed to be performed on Friday is an act which every one who has experienced a nocturnal pollution should prefer and approve.
A verb expressing an event as a positive fact
] is such, for instance, as in the phrase

body.text_bw: bayonamaA A^anaA ka*aA A_i*o jaA='a zayodN
body.text_ar: بَيْنَمَا اَنَا كَذَا اـِذْ جَا=ءَ زَيْدٌ

body.text: [while I was thus, or in [this state, lo, or behold, Zeyd came]. (S, L, art.
